_comment: Fixture stem -> the HTTP method, path, and Content-Type the harness replays in a burst against your endpoint. A rate-limit test is about the request COUNT within a window, not the body shape, so the bundled fixtures are representative REQUEST TEMPLATES: api_ping is a cheap GET probe (the default — body is not sent on a GET), api_write is a POST body for exercising a write-side limiter. The path is the endpoint you want to throttle-test; override --fixture or edit MANIFEST to point at your own route. Bodies are docs-derived (illustrative shapes with clearly-fake demo markers), never wire-captured — see PROVENANCE.md.

api_ping.note: primary — a cheap GET probe fired in a burst (default fixture)

api_write.note: a write-side POST burst — for limiters that scope by method/route
